revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Jōyama No.1 Kofun | native_name = 城山1号墳 | location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|qid=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba|1=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.859027777777776|N|140.58988888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | built = Mid-6th century | burial = {{ill|Side-entry stone chamber (Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entry stone chamber (Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with wooden coffin | burial goods = {{ill|Sankakuen shinjukyo mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen shinjukyo mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, decorated swords, haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | Historic Site1 = Chiba Prefectural designation1 | map = Japan Chiba | map_caption = location in Chiba Prefecture | image = [[File:Joyama Kofun Group (Katori)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Relocated stone chamber (preserved in Jōyama Park) }}
'''Jōyama No.1 Kofun''' (城山1号墳) was an '''ancient Kofun''' ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in '''{{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|qid=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba|1=Katori, Chiba}}, Japan'''. It was a '''keyhole-shaped tumulus (Zenpō-kōen-fun, 前方後円墳)''' and was '''the largest mound in the Jōyama Kofun Group (城山古墳群)'''.
The original '''mound no longer exists''', but '''the burial chamber has been relocated and preserved in Jōyama Park'''. burial goods from the tomb, including '''a [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankakuen Shinjukyo mirror]] and decorated swords''', are Historic Site1 '''Chiba Prefectural Historic Site'''.
== Overview == Jōyama No.1 Kofun was built on a plateau '''(elevation: 42 meters)''' on the '''south bank of the Kurobe River''', a tributary of the '''Tonegawa River''' (typeerly the Kinugawa). The plateau, known as '''Jōyama ("Castle Mountain")''', contains over '''20 Kofuns''', typeing the '''Jōyama Kofun Group'''.
Excavations were conducted in '''1963''', revealing an '''abundance of grave goods''', but the mound was subsequently '''destroyed'''.
== burial == Jōyama No.1 Kofun was a '''68-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus''' with its '''front portion facing north-northwest'''. The mound featured: * '''Terrace areas''' with '''[[Cylindrical Haniwa|cylindrical haniwa]]''' and '''[[Representational Haniwa|figurative haniwa]]''' (house-shaped, human, and horse-shaped) * '''Clay-lined ritual areas''' on the rear circular mound, where '''{{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (須恵器) and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} (土師器)''' were found * '''A surrounding moat (Shūkō, 周溝)'''
The '''burial chamber''' was a '''single-sided entry (片袖式) [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|horizontal stone chamber]]''' with a '''[[Wooden Coffin|wooden coffin]]'''.
Excavated '''grave goods''' include '''five ring-pommeled swords (Kantō-tachi, 環頭大刀)''', '''a sword with a club-shaped pommel (Tōtsui-tachi, 頭椎大刀)''', and '''a round-pommeled sword (Entō-tachi, 円頭大刀)'''.
=== '''Significance of the Sankakuen Shinjukyo Mirror''' === A '''[[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|Sankakuen Shinjukyo mirror]]''' was discovered in the burial chamber. This type of mirror is '''typically associated with Early Kofun built (3rd-4th century) tombs''', making its presence in a '''6th-century tomb highly unusual'''.
The same '''mirror mold''' (dōban, 銅版) was used to produce mirrors found in: * '''[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun|Nishikumezuka Kofun]] (西求女塚古墳)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture) * '''[[Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (椿井大塚山古墳)''' (Kyoto Prefecture) * '''An unknown kofun in Gifu Prefecture''' (possibly Tamoko Kofun, 頼母子古墳)
These mirrors were originally '''cast in China''' and later '''brought to Japan''' in the '''3rd century''', suggesting that '''Jōyama No.1 Kofun's ruling elite had access to heirloom burial goods that had been preserved for over 300 years'''.<ref>[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/smph/culture_sport/bunkazai/isan/isan_vol041-050.html#cmsVol041 "Archived Katori Heritage vol.041: Sankakuen Shinjukyo Mirror"], Katori City Official Website.</ref>
== burial Chamber == The '''stone chamber''' has been '''relocated to Jōyama Park'''. The original '''area''' were:<ref name="explanation">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/smph/culture_sport/bunkazai/isan/isan_vol031-040.html#cmsVol034 "Archived Katori Heritage vol.034: Jōyama No.1 Kofun"], Katori City Official Website.</ref> * '''Main chamber (gen-shitsu, 玄室)''': ** Length: '''4.5 meters''' ** Width: '''1.7 meters''' ** Height: '''1.5 meters''' * '''Entrance passage (sen-dō, 羨道)''': ** Length: '''2.2 meters''' ** Width: '''0.9 meters'''
The '''walls were made of stacked stones''', with '''large capstones''' over 1 meter in size.
== burial goods == '''Key excavated burial goods''' include:<ref name="chiba">[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p181-010.html "Jōyama No.1 Kofun burial goods"], Chiba Prefecture Education Board.</ref>
=== '''Bronze Mirror''' === * '''Sankakuen Shinjukyo Mirror (三角縁三神五獣鏡)''' ** Diameter: '''22.2 cm''' ** Contains '''42-character inscription''', beginning with '''吾作明竟 ("I made this bright and clear")'''
=== '''Ornaments''' === * Glass beads (Tonbo-dama, 蜻蛉玉) * Silver beads (Ginkū-dama, 銀空玉) * Gold earrings * Gilded bronze crown?
=== '''Weapons''' === * '''Five Ring-Pommeled Swords (環頭大刀)''' * '''Club-Pommeled Sword (頭椎大刀)''' * '''Round-Pommeled Sword (円頭大刀)''' * '''Daggers, arrowheads'''
=== '''Armor and Equipment''' === * '''Iron Helmet with Riveted Plates (横矧板鋲留衝角付冑)''' * '''Lamellar Armor (挂甲, Keikō)''' * '''Shin Guards (臑当, Sune-ate)'''
=== '''Horse Equipment''' === * '''Iron Bit with Gilded Plaque (鉄地金銅張鏡板付轡)''' * '''Stirrups (鐙)''' * '''Sa11le and decorative fittings (鞍, 杏葉)'''
<gallery> File:城山1号墳 (香取市) 出土 三角縁三神五獣鏡 (複製).JPG|Sankakuen Shinjukyo Mirror (replica) File:城山1号墳 (香取市) 出土 装飾付大刀.JPG|Decorated Sword File:城山1号墳 (香取市) 出土 馬具.JPG|Horse Equipment </gallery>
== Historic Site ==
=== '''Chiba Prefectural designation1''' === * '''Jōyama No.1 Kofun burial goods''' ** '''Designated:''' April 18, 1969 ** '''Stored at:''' '''Katori City designation1 Preservation Center'''
== Related Sites == * '''{{ill|Jōyama Kofun Group|ja|城山古墳群 (香取市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyama Kofun Group|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|qid=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}'''
==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p181-010.html Jōyama No.1 Kofun burial goods] (Chiba Prefecture Website)
